# Cycle 368 Review

## 1. [worklog-accuracy] The “auto-generated” receipt table still drops both pre-close-out dispatch commits

**File**: `docs/worklog/2026-03-26/083215-cycle-368-review-processing-audit-acceptance-dual-dispatch.md:37-50`
**Evidence**: The worklog says the table was “auto-generated by `cycle-receipts --cycle 368`”. But the published table lists only eight receipts and omits two dispatch commits: `1643d710` (`state(record-dispatch): #1804 dispatched [cycle 368]`, 2026-03-26 08:29:07 UTC) and `c460962c` (`state(record-dispatch): #1805 dispatched [cycle 368]`, 2026-03-26 08:29:30 UTC). Both commits are earlier than the first `cycle-complete` commit, `1b714bd9` (08:31:49 UTC), so they are part of the in-scope cycle-complete snapshot rather than a later correction. Running `bash tools/cycle-receipts --cycle 368 --repo-root .` on the current tree returns those receipts too. That makes the artifact’s “auto-generated” claim untrustworthy even before the later reconciliation and metrics-fix commits are considered.
**Recommendation**: Make the worklog receipt section consume the exact same receipt stream as `cycle-receipts`, and add a regression check that pre-`cycle-complete` `state(record-dispatch)` commits must appear unless the note explicitly says they are excluded.

## 2. [state-integrity] `last_cycle.summary` was overwritten with a zero-activity summary even though the cycle merged one PR and dispatched two follow-ups

**File**: `docs/state.json:5726-5731`
**Evidence**: `last_cycle.summary` says `"0 dispatches, 0 merges"`. But the same file records a merged cycle-review session for issue `#1800` / PR `#1801` at `docs/state.json:5191-5197`. It also records two in-flight follow-up dispatches, `#1804` and `#1805`, at `docs/state.json:5200-5210`, and `copilot_metrics.dispatch_log_latest` points at `#1805` with `in_flight: 2` at `docs/state.json:5429-5442`. The worklog independently reports the merge and both dispatches at `docs/worklog/2026-03-26/083215-cycle-368-review-processing-audit-acceptance-dual-dispatch.md:5-10`. This is not just stale prose. Commit `95e6e8f2` replaced the accurate cycle-complete summary from `1b714bd9` with the false zero-activity text at the same 08:35:04 timestamp that the merged session was recorded.
**Recommendation**: Derive `last_cycle.summary` from the actual cycle delta (merged sessions plus new dispatches) and refuse to write a generic zero-activity summary when `agent_sessions` or the cycle worklog show real activity for that cycle.

## 3. [review-accounting] The cycle marked the prior process-adherence finding as `dispatch_created` without creating the promised fail-closed fix issue

**File**: `docs/state.json:9188-9207`
**Evidence**: The review-history entry for cycle 367 says `dispatch_created: 2` and explicitly records `F1 dispatch_created: will dispatch close_out.rs C5.5 fail-closed fix`. But the cycle’s actual follow-up issues are `#1803` (audit acknowledgment for auto-injecting `--addresses-finding`), `#1804` (C6.5 pipeline field fix), and `#1805` (record-dispatch wrapper auto-detection). None of those issues covers the promised C5.5 fail-closed repair. The current `agent_sessions` tail also shows only the two in-flight dispatches for `#1804` and `#1805` at `docs/state.json:5200-5210`. So the hardest prior finding was counted as dispatched in review accounting without an actual dispatch artifact to point at.
**Recommendation**: Only mark a finding `dispatch_created` after the follow-up issue exists, and store the issue number alongside the disposition so review-history cannot claim a dispatch that has no corresponding issue.

## Complacency score

**2/5** — The cycle did keep an audit trail: issue `#1802` has 27 cycle-368 step comments, and both `bash tools/state-invariants` and `bash tools/metric-snapshot` pass on the current tree. But three core accountability artifacts still misstate basic facts: the worklog’s mechanical receipt table is incomplete, `state.json` says the cycle had zero dispatches and zero merges when it did both, and review-history claims a fail-closed follow-up was dispatched when no such issue exists. That is not isolated sloppiness; it is repeat accuracy drift in the exact areas the cycle claimed were being tightened.
